I spent the last few weeks fine-tuning an LLM with @UnslothAI

The model turns vague trading strategy ideas into strict, executable backtest configs.

Most LLMs are great at talking about strategies, but terrible at giving deterministic backend-valid JSON that can actually run without errors.

My initial setup:

• 7B param model
• ~2,200 training examples
• QLoRA fine-tuning
• RTX 4060 Ti (16gb VRAM)

I wanted to see how far I could push an open model on this.

The model is far from perfect, but it's already better than the base model at generating backend-valid backtest configs

Next steps:

• add real production configs
• increase dataset size
• improve reference vs. value accuracy
• compare against larger models

As I train more versions, I'll track results and evals in a benchmark.

More soon.

tweets.full_text:
This type of analysis is just complete b/s, statistically.

To break it down:

(1) In a typical year - the one I link (2023) is totally typical - there will be 5-6,000,000 serious Index Violent Crimes and up to 20M total crimes. Some small percentage of these crimes will be inter-racial: in 2023, there were 385K B-on-W Index VC and 118K W-on-B Index VC out of ~6M serious crimes.

(2) So, rhe actual B:W ratio here is  3.27:1. 

(3) To get the crazy ratios we see online, you multiply by (6) to reflect the smaller size of the Black population. This is what the "per 10,000 people" stuff below is about. This gets you a ratio of "19.62 to 1," within this total tiny set of 500K/6M crimes. 

(4) But, this is dishonest absent one other step. There are also 5-6x as many white potential TARGETS - ~76% of the population with Spanish folX in - so that a totally anti-racist criminal would rob whites 600% as often as Blacks. In fact, absent higher Black crime rates, you'd EXPECT almost identical numbers of W-on-B and B-on-W crimes - just ran this through STATA and AI - because (6 x 1/6) = (1/6 x 6).

Adding in this 2nd adjustment gets us basically back to the starting point: 117k, 385k, 6M, and 3:1. Not doing so and picking an atypical year gets you silliness like "50: 1."

It's worth noting that you can play games in all directions with this data. For example, in 2023, Blacks (117/40M) were in fact significantly more likely than Caucasians (385/200+M) to be VICTIMS of an inter-racial Index crime.

As vs trading bank stats, let's lock up or kill criminals.

tweets.full_text:
True story —

The first week I started using AI last month, I automated my (at that point) existing system which I'll call "manual mechanical."

There wasn't really any discretion in it, but it qualifies as discretion because I was executing manually. I would count down the seconds on candle closures (watching a seconds-based clock app on my phone) and execute 1-2 seconds before the new candle opened.

It was basically a perfect candidate for automation.

I also considered it to be last 20% or so of the model that would be too complex to automate without hiring an MT5 programmer. I'd already automated my trailing stops as well as time-of-entry (I knew when I had to be at the charts to see whether I had a setup). I just figured that execution would need me for a while longer.

Until recently, I hadn't realised how far AI has come as far as actually writing EA-code without too many bugs; not to mention the brainstorming and testing strengths.

I signed up for Claude and got to work building the 100%-automated version of that model I was manually executing.

I'd called this system "candle open manipulation" (COM), and I'd created a visual diagram of the logic using drawing tools in TV, refining and clarifying the reasoning why the logic would be profitable. I screen-capped the drawing every time I made a change. Months and months of work.

I'd backtested exhaustively in TV (as far as manual backtesting anyway) and couldn't wait to fully automate after discovering it was possible sooner than I'd thought.

It only took me a few days to build because writing the logic in English I'd already worked through over and over was pretty simple. I was satisfied with the final EA and now felt energized at the prospect of having my plan execute without disrupting the day.

In the Summer, I jump on a ferry to visit my parents in France. Typically, it's frustrating to miss out on market action while I'm at sea. Not any more!

Only one problem though...

More vigorous testing methods enabled through AI (and MT5-testing vs TV-only through manual means) revealed that my system wasn't actually anywhere near as profitable as I'd thought. Oh shit.

I double-confirmed the results and even backtested the reverse version of the model. Turned out that over a longer period of time, my system was extremely noisy, and no where near what I'd term robust.

AI had made it clear at an alarming pace just how much of the picture I'd been missing over the years.

I sulked for a couple of hours and then realised that I could have wasted the rest of the year trading the same way, so got back to it and spent the next 3 weeks building a portfolio-based system from the ground up, with robustness and multi-regime sensitivity in mind. 

No more trading one model on one market and expecting it to work exactly the same every year🤦‍♂️

I brainstormed with the AI using logic from the landfill of previous systems I've used, as well as general market observations I've gathered, to figure out where edge might exist. I've never been short of ideas. The difficulty in trading for me has usually been a lack of rigor in testing, which AI now effectively solved for.

It's still surreal how much of a change this has been from my 6 years of trading. Going from TV nearly every day, to not having opened it once for a couple of weeks now.

AI isn't perfect. It's like being shortsighted and needing glasses to correct for that. AI will hallucinate things. But in the bigger picture, it's a pretty minor inconvenience compared to what it brings to the table. ("I wear glasses but look, I have bionic legs!")

Traders still *not* using AI might not have to wear glasses, but it's suddenly become clearer how freakin' hard trading was pre-AI with our own mental faculties alone to process reams of data; expecting to execute that task as efficiently as a machine, with low noise generation.

Never has it been clearer to me how unsuitable the human brain is for that task.

AI has made me realise I'd spent years thinking my eyesight was 20-20, but actually I'd been blind all along, my hands had been tied behind my back, and I'd been wading through mud.

But hey, on the other hand, maybe I just needed to add one more "90% WR" model from YT with some dude's premium indicator and I would have been set for life? Guess we'll never know.

---コピペ▼

以下の問いに対して、1回の回答の中で「深度優先探索アルゴリズム」を模倣して思考を展開してください。

【探索ルール】
- 可能性のある解候補を列挙してから1つを選んで深掘りする
- 深掘りした結果が行き詰まった場合は「❌ 行き詰まり」と明示してバックトラックする
- バックトラック後は次の候補を選んで再探索する
- 解が見つかった場合は「✅ 解発見」と明示して探索を終了する

【探索フォーマット】
候補リスト：[A, B, C, D...]

→ 候補Aを深掘り：

　　└ 論理展開②

　　└ ...

【最終出力】
探索で発見した最良の解をもとに、ユーザーへの回答を出力してください。

外注先が同じなら、似たような結果しか得られないのは、当然と言える。

すべての学生がGPT-4にエッセイを外注し、すべてのプログラマーがCopilotにコードを外注し、すべてのマーケターが同じAIにキャッチコピーを外注する。

個々を見れば、どれも減点のない、それっぽい成果物だ。しかし、全員が同じ統計モデルという外注先を共有しているため、市場や教室に出回るアウトプットは不気味なほど均質化する。

プロンプトによる差別化は表面的なカスタマイズになっても、本質的な意味領域の拡張にはならない。LLMという巨大な多次元の統計空間（過去のデータの圧縮物）の中から、指定された条件に合う確率のルートを、ただトリミングしているだけだからだ。

「もっと創造的にするよう促したり、連鎖的思考（CoT）プロンプトを使ったりしても、同質化の効果は残った」

サイコロの振り方をどれだけ工夫しても、サイコロの目の総数自体が増えるわけではないのだ。

さらに、自分だけの秘密のプロンプトを作って差別化しようとするレース自体が、新自由主義的なプラットフォームのアルゴリズムに一瞬で回収される。

「こうプロンプトを打てば、他と違う成果物が出る」

というハックの手法が見つかった瞬間、それはSNSでバズり、まとめられ、最終的にはAI自身が『最強のプロンプト』を生成し、誰もがボタン一つで使える機能としてシステムに取り込まれる。

他者と差別化するための独自のノイズ（創造性）を効率化の名のもとにドブに捨てた結果、残るのは誰もが同じように賢く、誰もが同じように凡庸な、価値ゼロのコモディティ空間だけだ。

quoted_status.full_text:
Students without access to LLMs are 2 to 8 times more creative than students with access.

That is the finding of a new paper comparing 2,200 college admissions essays written by humans before ChatGPT with essays generated by GPT-4.

The key point is not individual creativity. GPT-4 can write well, sometimes better than individual students. The problem is collective creativity.

Each new human essay added new semantic territory. New ideas. New angles. New experiences. New combinations.

Each new GPT-4 essay added much less.

The authors call this the diversity growth rate: how much novelty each additional text contributes to the collective pool of ideas.

Humans kept expanding the pool. GPT-4 made the pool converge.

Even when the authors pushed GPT-4 to be more creative, changed parameters, or used chain-of-thought prompting, the homogenizing effect remained.

This is the real danger of AI in education.
Not that students will write worse.
That everyone will write the same.

*

tweets.full_text:
**Nexusさん、こんにちは！**  
手伝いますよ🙌 状況を整理してアドバイスしますね。

### 現在のX収益化の主な条件（2026年6月時点）
- 過去3ヶ月で**合計500万インプレッション**（オーガニック）
- **Premium（認証済み）フォロワー500人以上**
- 自分自身もX Premium加入
- Stripe設定・本人確認完了

一度500万到達したのに収益化が始まらない主な理由は以下の通りです。

### よくある原因
1. **「総インプレッション」ではなく「Premiumインプレッション」が重要**  
   最近は**Premiumユーザーがホーム画面で見る回数**が収益のほとんどを決めます。  
   総インプが500万あっても、無料ユーザーが多いと収益化がスムーズに進みません。

2. **リーチ減少で3ヶ月累計が500万を下回った可能性**  
   一度達成しても、最近投稿が減ったりエンゲージメントが落ちると資格がリセットされやすいです。

3. **AI生成画像の多用**  
   AI画像が目立つと「非オリジナルコンテンツ」と判断されやすく、アルゴリズムからリーチを抑えられたり、Premiumユーザーに推されにくくなります。  
   → 収益化が遠のく大きな要因の一つです。

### すぐに試してほしい対策
- **AI画像を減らす**：自分で撮影した写真・動画中心に切り替える（どうしても使う場合は「AI生成です」と必ず明記）
- **毎日1〜2投稿を継続**（質問を入れて反応を促す）
  例：「皆さんは雨の季節にどんな癒しを求めていますか？」「おすすめのリラックス方法教えてください」など
- **動画を少し混ぜる**（短いものでOK）
- 設定 → 収益化 で現在のステータスを確認

私は、癒し系の路線がとても良いと思います！  
オリジナルコンテンツにシフトして継続すれば、500万インプレッションもPremium露出も必ず積み上がりますよ✨

もしアナリティクス画面や収益化設定画面の画像があれば、さらに詳しく見ます！  
一緒に頑張りましょう🙏
